open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW, scope extended this tick. No numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary. Three ingest boundaries now share the same uncapped-cumulative-bytes pattern: merge.js addFiles() (queued PDFs), and as of this tick img2pdf.js createPdf() (queued images — fresh user-picked image files, a genuine NEW producer-side ingest boundary; per-image 50MB + magic-byte check present, but no cap on total queued image bytes). All are client-side-only DoS (uploader's own tab) on the 1.6 GiB box. Developer task — bound total queued bytes at each ingest boundary and add the page-count cap once at viewer load to cover all per-page consumers. extract-pages.js (also new this tick) is NOT a new load boundary: it reads only the already-validated OPEN doc via currentDoc.getData(); its numeric-only parser is bounded against numPages (duplicates allowed -> output can multiply, same LOW self-DoS family, no separate cap needed). No new server-side risk.

notes: 38 JS modules (+2 since 20:20 review: img2pdf.js, extract-pages.js). img2pdf.js (TASK-346) — builds a PDF from picked JPEG/PNG images, one page each, downloaded; nothing uploaded, viewer doc untouched. Security CLEAN: type decided by MAGIC BYTES (FF D8 FF / PNG 89504E47..., not the spoofable File.type), per-image 50MB cap + empty reject, filenames sanitized, status via textContent (zero innerHTML), safe Blob download with rel=noopener + URL.revokeObjectURL, PDFLib-unready guard, errors caught/surfaced via EventBus.ERROR. Only SEC-002-class note: no cap on cumulative queued image bytes (folded into SEC-002, same family as merge.js). NB TASK-346 is FAILED on a UX bug (img2pdf drop handler omits e.stopPropagation, so upload.js window-level drop also fires a spurious 'must be .pdf' toast) — developer fix, NOT a security defect; both drop paths validate safely. extract-pages.js — keeps an arbitrary ordered/duplicated page set from the OPEN doc via currentDoc.getData()->PDFLib.load()->copyPages->save; NOT a new load boundary; parser numeric-only (^\d+$ / ^\d+-\d+$) bounded against numPages; textContent only; filename sanitized [a-zA-Z0-9._-].slice(0,180)->_extracted.pdf; safe Blob download+revoke; PDFLib/no-doc guards; errors caught. Full-JS audit across all 38 modules: every innerHTML grep hit is a COMMENT (no innerHTML with user/PDF data anywhere); zero eval/Function/document.write/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML; zero fetch/XHR/importScripts/remote-src; no hardcoded secrets. upload.js still enforces ext+MIME+empty+size(50MB)+%PDF- magic with sanitized filenames. Live headers verified: full resource CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; worker-src 'self' blob:; img-src 'self' blob: data:; connect-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; font-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. nginx -t OK. Path blocking: .git/config 403, CLAUDE.md 403, js/img2pdf.js 200, js/extract-pages.js 200. No failed/invalid SSH auth in last 24h. Disk 44% used, 7.9G free.
